title: The Monk
author: Matthew Lewis
description: Matthew Lewis's novel "The Monk" stands as a seminal work of Gothic literature, intricately weaving themes of forbidden desire, morality, and the supernatural. Published in 1796, this provocative text employs a compelling narrative style that blends the sensational with the philosophical, challenging the austere conventions of its time. Through the tragic descent of Ambrosio, a pious monk entangled in lust and ultimately in demonic influence, Lewis critiques the hypocrisy of religious institutio
subjects: British and irish fiction (fictional works by one author)
